name: Lisbon Portuguese
notes: Lisbon / Estremenho (Centro-Litoral) European Portuguese — the prestige REFERENCE variety. Cintra (1971; Boletim de Filologia 22:81-116) shows the standard, prestige norm of European Portuguese IS essentially the Central-Southern / Lisbon (Estremenho) variety, so the pt-PT base already models nearly all of Lisbon: strong unstressed vowel reduction (/e/->[ɨ]), dark coda /l/->[ɫ], coda-sibilant chiado /s z/->[ʃ ʒ], and the coda-s sandhi are ALL inherited from pt-PT and are NOT restated here (they are the standard = Lisbon). The only positional rows kept are the unstressed /a/->[ɐ] and /o/->[u] reduction entries — retained (rather than left to the base) so Lisbon reduces the unstressed mid/low vowels without pulling in the base's open [ɔ]/[a] stressed-nucleus defaults, which are lexically unpredictable for spelling-unmarked stressed <o>/<a>. The genuine, well-sourced Lisbon-specific deltas over that Lisbon-type base are minimal: (1) <ei> -> [ɐj] — the diagnostic Lisbon/Estremenho lowering-centralisation of the falling diphthong, where more conservative/Northern EP keeps [ej] (leite -> [ˈlɐjtɨ], primeiro -> [pɾiˈmɐjɾu], reino -> [ˈʁɐjnu], seis -> [ˈsɐjʃ]). Cintra (1971) and Segura (2013) treat the [ɐj] realisation as characteristic of the Centro-Litoral/Estremenho zone; Mateus & d'Andrade (2000) transcribe the standard <ei> as [ɐj]; (2) <ou> -> [o] — complete monophthongisation, general in the Central-Southern standard where Northern varieties keep the diphthong [ow] (ouro -> [ˈoɾu]); (3) rhotic realised as the uvular fricative [ʁ] (the Lisbon-urban norm) and unstressed reduced schwa surfacing as [ɨ]. KNOWN LIMIT (documented, not faked): a further reported Lisbon feature is the centralisation of stressed <e> to [ɐ] before a palatal (coelho, espelho, abelha); this conditioning is 'stressed vowel before a palatal consonant', a context the engine's positional vocabulary does not currently express (there is no before-palatal GraphemePosition), so it is left unmodelled rather than approximated with an over-applying rule. Intervocalic voiced-stop spirantisation (/b d ɡ/->[β ð ɣ]) is a general EP process left to a narrow-transcription task (see the pt-PT base notes). Because these deltas are already the values the pt-PT base is built around, Lisbon diverges from the base only in the <ei>/<ou> grapheme values and the narrowed rhotic/schwa allophones.

ancestors.notes: Standard (Lisbon-type) European Portuguese. Lisbon/Estremenho is the prestige reference variety and shares nearly all base processes; it diverges only by <ei>->[ɐj], <ou>->[o] and the narrowed rhotic/schwa allophones.

sources.author: Cintra, Luís F. Lindley
sources.title: Nova proposta de classificação dos dialectos galego-portugueses
sources.publisher: Boletim de Filologia (Centro de Estudos Filológicos, Lisboa), vol. 22, pp. 81-116
sources.notes: Foundational classification of the Galician-Portuguese dialect space by maximally diagnostic phonetic features. Establishes that the standard/prestige norm of European Portuguese coincides with the Central-Southern (Estremenho/Lisbon) variety, and that the [ɐj] value of the falling diphthong <ei> is characteristic of that Centro-Litoral zone as against the conservative [ej]. Republished in Cintra, Estudos de Dialectologia Portuguesa, Sá da Costa, 1983, pp. 117-163.

sources.author: Segura, Luísa
sources.title: Variedades dialetais do português europeu
sources.publisher: In Raposo, E.B.P. et al. (eds.), Gramática do Português, vol. I, Fundação Calouste Gulbenkian, Lisboa, pp. 85-142
sources.notes: Modern EP dialectology synthesis. Locates the Centro-Litoral/Estremenho (Lisbon) variety as the prestige standard and documents the [ɐj] realisation of <ei> and <ou> monophthongisation of the Central-Southern zone against the diphthong-preserving North.

sources.author: Mateus, M.H.M. & d'Andrade, E.
sources.title: The Phonology of Portuguese
sources.notes: Reference (Lisbon-standard) EP phonology; transcribes the standard falling diphthong <ei> as [ɐj] and documents the base processes (unstressed vowel reduction, dark coda /l/, coda sibilants) inherited unchanged from pt-PT.

sources.author: Cunha, C. & Cintra, L.F.L.
sources.title: Nova Gramática do Português Contemporâneo
sources.notes: Standard reference grammar describing the Lisbon prestige norm.

stress.notes: Acordo Ortográfico 1990 conventions: paroxytone default; written accents override; final -i/-u/-r/-l/-z/-x/-im/-om/-um/-ns and nasal diphthongs attract final stress. Unmarked -em/-am stay paroxytone (homem, falam).
